thesis: Global leader in grid-scale battery energy storage with record $5.5B backlog and 154% YoY revenue growth. Positioned as primary beneficiary of data center electrification, grid modernization, and IRA tax credits. Thin margins (4.9% gross) and cash burn create execution risk, but asymmetric upside if software mix expands and margins reach 10-15% target.

drivers: Data center electrification drives 2-3x demand acceleration
Software attach rate exceeds 40%, expanding blended margins to 15%+
International expansion captures Europe and Asia grid storage boom
Strategic M&A or partnership with hyperscaler validates platform

risks: Project delays and cost overruns compress margins below 8%
Competition from Tesla Megapack and BYD erodes market share
IRA tax credit repeal or delay slows U.S. demand
Cash burn accelerates, requiring dilutive equity raise

base_case.assumptions: $5.5B backlog converts over 2-3 years, software mix reaches 30%, gross margins expand to 12%, EBITDA margins reach 10% by FY2028. Market prices in $3B revenue run rate with improving profitability.

catalysts.event: Q2 FY2026 earnings — backlog and margin guidance update
FY2026 guidance update — full-year revenue and margin targets
Ongoing project announcements — data center partnerships, international wins

key_risks: Customer concentration in large utility projects creates revenue lumpiness
Cash burn rate of ~$50M/quarter implies 5-6 quarters of runway
Interest rate sensitivity increases project financing costs
Grid interconnection delays (3-5 year queue) defer revenue recognition
Commodity price volatility (lithium, cobalt, nickel) affects input costs
